free_text: Differential cross section measurement of the
natO(d,d0) reaction at energies and angles relevant
to EBS
The energy calibration of the accelerator was
performed using the 7Li(p,n) threshold reaction at
1880.4 keV. Maximum beam current 40-50 nA.
solid 16O target used for the measurements was made by
evaporating high purity SiO on a thin silver backing of
thickness (580 +/- 23) X 10**15 at/cm2.
Four 300 um-thick surface barrier detectors
located at 90, 135, 150 and 165 degree relative to the
incident beam.
The ration N(Ag)/N(O) was measured by the proton
elastic scattering spectra
(Ep = 2000 and 2500 keV) at the detection angles of
150 degree and 165 degree assuming the Rutherford
formula for the natAg(p,p0) elastic scattering and
considering evaluated cross section data for the
16O(p,p0) reaction.
The total uncertainty was determined from the
following partial uncertainties
Target O to Ag areal density ratio <3%.
O to Ag yield ratio <4%.
Numerical data received from Dr. O.Kakuee.
VS.
VT. ERR-ANALYS: ERR-1-> (ERR-1,,3.)
Plotted on FIgs.3a-3d of Nucl. Instr. Meth.
